---
title: "Simple phylogenetic tree plots (Open Tree of Life)"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
  pdf_document: default
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Setup and libraries

```{r,message=F,warning=F}
# Simple phylogenetic tree plots for CryoZoo species
# One circular tree per common_group, with IUCN status as outer ring
# Run interactively in RStudio — not via Rscript

library(tidyverse)
library(rotl)
library(ape)
library(ggtree)
library(ggtreeExtra)
library(rredlist)
library(rphylopic)
```

## Paths

```{r}
# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
path     <- here::here()
out_dir  <- file.path(path, "output", "figures", "trees")
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

## IUCN colours

```{r}
# ── IUCN colours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
IUCN_COLOURS <- c(
  LC  = "#60C659",
  NT  = "#CCE226",
  VU  = "#F9E814",
  EN  = "#FC7F3F",
  CR  = "#D81E05",
  EW  = "#542344",
  EX  = "#000000",
  DD  = "#D1D1C6",
  NE  = "#AAAAAA"
)

```

## 1. Load & tidy species table

```{r}
# ── 1. Load & tidy species table ──────────────────────────────────────────────
species_list <- readRDS(file.path(path, "output", "Species_List_with_genome_and_cl_info.rds")) %>%
  mutate(
    common_group = case_when(
      class %in% c("Actinopteri", "Chondrichthyes") ~ "Fish",
      class %in% c("Lepidosauria")                  ~ "Reptilia",
      is.na(class)                                  ~ "Reptilia",
      TRUE                                           ~ class
    ),
    # Fix known name mismatches for OTL
    species_TOL = case_match(
      species,
      "Astur gentilis"    ~ "Accipiter gentilis",
      "Caribicus warreni" ~ "Celestus warreni",
      .default = species
    )
  )

cat("Species per common_group:\n")
print(count(species_list, common_group))

```

## Fetch IUCN status from the Red List API

```{r}
# Check / set your IUCN Red List API token (get one at https://api.iucnredlist.org)
# Set IUCN_KEY in your .Renviron (usethis::edit_r_environ()) rather than hardcoding it here.

iucn_key <- Sys.getenv("IUCN_KEY")
if (iucn_key == "") stop("IUCN_KEY is not set. Add it to your .Renviron (see usethis::edit_r_environ()).")
iucn_fetch<- species_list %>%
  mutate(
    iucn_status = map_chr(species, function(sname) {
      parts <- str_split(sname, " ")[[1]]
      res <- tryCatch(
        rl_species(genus = parts[1], species = parts[2], key = iucn_key)$assessments,
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
      if (is.null(res) || !is.data.frame(res) || nrow(res) == 0) return(NA_character_)
      res %>%
        slice_max(assessment_id, n = 1,with_ties = F) %>%
        pull(red_list_category_code)
    })) %>%
  select(species,iucn_status) %>%
  mutate(iucn_status = iucn_status %>%
           na_if("NA") %>%
           recode(
             `LR/lc` = "LC",
             `V`     = "VU",
             `I`     = "DD"
           ) %>%
           factor(levels = names(IUCN_COLOURS))
  )

unique(iucn_fetch$iucn_status)

species_list<- species_list %>%
  left_join(iucn_fetch)
```

## 2. Fetch OTL tree for a set of species

```{r}
# ── 2. Fetch OTL tree for a set of species ────────────────────────────────────
fetch_tree <- function(species_vec, group_name) {
  message("Fetching OTL tree for ", group_name, " (", length(species_vec), " spp)...")

  matched <- tryCatch(
    tnrs_match_names(species_vec, context_name = NULL),
    error = function(e) { message("  OTL match failed: ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(matched)) return(NULL)

  ott_ids <- matched %>%
    filter(!is.na(ott_id), !flags %in% c("barren", "extinct")) %>%
    pull(ott_id) %>%
    unique()

  if (length(ott_ids) < 3) {
    message("  Too few resolved names (", length(ott_ids), "), skipping")
    return(NULL)
  }

  tree <- tryCatch(
    tol_induced_subtree(ott_ids = ott_ids, label_format = "name"),
    error = function(e) { message("  Subtree fetch failed: ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(tree)) return(NULL)

  tree$tip.label <- tree$tip.label %>%
    str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
    str_remove("\\s+ott\\d+$")

  tree <- compute.brlen(tree, method = "Grafen")
  tree
}

```

## 3. Build one circular tree plot with IUCN ring

```{r}
# ── 3. Build one circular tree plot with IUCN ring ────────────────────────────
build_class_plot <- function(tree, meta_df, class_name) {

  tip_df <- tibble(species = tree$tip.label) %>%
    left_join(meta_df, by = "species")

  iucn_df <- tip_df %>%
    select(species, iucn_status) %>%
    mutate(iucn_status = factor(iucn_status, levels = names(IUCN_COLOURS)))

  # ── Base tree ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- ggtree(tree, layout = "circular", size = 0.35, colour = "grey40") +
    labs(title = class_name) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11,
                                     hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 4)),
      legend.position = "none"
    )

  #Get node for each qualifying order and label it
  order_counts <- tip_df %>%
    count(order) %>%
    filter(n >= 3)

  if (nrow(order_counts)>0){
    order_nodes <- order_counts %>%
      pull(order) %>%
      set_names() %>%
      map_int(function(ord) {
        tips <- tip_df %>%
          filter(order == ord) %>%
          pull(species) %>%
          intersect(tree$tip.label)
        if (length(tips) < 2) return(NA_integer_)
        getMRCA(tree, tips)
      }) %>%
      enframe(name = "order", value = "node") %>%
      filter(!is.na(node)) %>%
      left_join(order_counts, by = "order")

    orders <- unique(order_nodes$order)
    my_colours <- setNames(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(nrow(order_nodes), "Paired"), orders)
    # then map colour = my_colours[order] inside aes, or join back onto order_nodes
    order_nodes$clade_col <- my_colours[order_nodes$order]

    p <- p +
      geom_cladelab(
        data        = order_nodes,
        mapping     = aes(node = node, label = order, colour= clade_col),
        fontsize    = 8,
        fontface    = "italic",
        offset      = 0.0,   # tweak to sit just outside tip labels
        offset.text = -0.5,
        barsize     = 1.5,
        angle = "auto"
      )+
      scale_colour_identity()
  }

  # ── IUCN ring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- p +
    geom_fruit(
      data        = iucn_df,
      geom        = geom_tile,
      mapping     = aes(y = species, fill = iucn_status),
      width       = 0.04,
      offset      = 0.06,
      colour      = "white",
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = IUCN_COLOURS, na.value = "grey90",
                      name = "IUCN Status", drop = FALSE)




  # ── Tip labels ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 # p <- p +
  #  geom_tiplab(size = 1.8, offset = 0.12,
   #             hjust = 0, fontface = "italic", colour = "grey20")
}
```

## 4. Run for each common_group

```{r}
# ── 4. Run for each common_group ──────────────────────────────────────────────
groups <- unique(species_list$common_group)

for (grp in groups) {
  #grp<-"Fish"
  sp_vec <- species_list %>%
    filter(common_group == grp) %>%
    pull(species_TOL)

  tree <- fetch_tree(sp_vec, grp)
  if (is.null(tree)) {
    message("Skipping ", grp, " — no tree returned")
    next
  }

  p <- build_class_plot(tree, species_list, grp)

  # Size plot by number of species
  n   <- length(tree$tip.label)
  dim <- max(8, round(sqrt(n) * 1.5))

  out_file <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("tree_nolabels_", grp, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_file, p, width = dim, height = dim, units = "in", device = png)
  message("Saved: ", out_file)
}

message("\nDone! All trees saved to: ", out_dir)
```

## Legend

```{r}
legend<-tibble(
  status = factor(names(IUCN_COLOURS), levels = names(IUCN_COLOURS))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = status, y = 1, fill = status)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = IUCN_COLOURS, drop = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "right",
    legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "IUCN Status"))

ggsave(paste0(out_dir,"/legend.png"), legend, width = 5, height = 10, units = "in", device = png)
```
